filter_pushdown.note: Most selective predicate — applied first; also drives zone map pruning

notes: No joins, no subqueries, no GROUP BY — pure scan-filter-aggregate
Zone map on l_shipdate skips ~60-70% of 600 blocks before any row-level work
All three filters fused into single pass over qualifying blocks
Aggregation produces exactly 1 output row (no hash table needed)

data_structures.qualifying_blocks: flat_array<uint32_t> of block indices (~180-240 entries, built in zone map phase)
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local_int64_scalar[64] — one accumulator per thread, no hash table

zone_map_phase.execution: sequential single-threaded pre-pass, ~14KB metadata — negligible cost

scan_phase.scan_scope: qualifying blocks only (identified by zone map phase)
scan_phase.simd_strategy: AVX2 — 8x int32 per instruction for l_shipdate comparisons; 4x int64 for l_discount and l_quantity; combine masks with bitwise AND
scan_phase.accumulation: for qualifying rows: thread_local_sum += l_extendedprice[i] * l_discount[i]
scan_phase.precision_note: Accumulate raw int64 product (scale=10000); output = final_sum / 10000 for SQL value. Alternatively: per-row divide by 100 then final divide by 100 as per query guide formula.

parallelism.morsel_unit: one block = 100K rows (aligns with zone map block boundaries)
parallelism.block_distribution: atomic fetch_add over qualifying_blocks array — threads pull next block index dynamically
parallelism.partition_count: N/A — no partitioning needed (scalar accumulator, no hash contention)
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_scalar_merge — each thread accumulates into private int64; single sequential merge of 64 values after parallel phase
parallelism.expected_blocks_per_thread: ~3-4 qualifying blocks per thread (200 blocks / 64 threads)

physical_plan.memory_pattern: streaming_sequential — columnar blocks read left-to-right within each qualifying block; no random access
physical_plan.no_materialization: qualifying rows never written to memory — extendedprice*discount accumulated directly into register; result is a single int64 scalar

estimated_cost.bottleneck: memory_bandwidth (4 columns x ~20M rows x 8 bytes = ~640MB, streamed across 64 cores)
estimated_cost.target_comparison: Umbra=14ms, DuckDB=20ms; this plan targets ~15-20ms via zone map + 64-thread parallelism + fused branch-free SIMD filter
